titleStmt.title: Letter from Rebecca Hime to Ephraim MacDowell Cosgrave, 11 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Rebecca Hime

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Rebecca Hime to Ephraim MacDowell Cosgrave (1853-1925) regarding a portrait of her father, James Apjohn (1796-1886). Rebecca describes the portrait and explains that the portrait was to be donated to the college following her death.
Ephraim MacDowel Cosgrave served as president of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland in 1914 and held the position until 1916. Aside from his dedication to medicine Cosgrave was also a member of the Royal Society of Antiquities of Ireland, treasurer of the Royal Zoological Society of Ireland and a founder and secretary of the Georgian Society of Dublin.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HILLMOUNT,
LONDONDERRY. 11.3.16 Dear Sir In 1875 the friends and former pupils
of my Father, (the late James Apjohn M.D.F.R.S
Professor of Chemistry in T.C.D,) presented
him with his portrait on his retiring from
that post, the presentation took place in
large hall of College of Physicians in December
of that year. The picture is a very fine one,
and an excellent likeness, the Artist being
the late Sir Thos Jones. It measures 6.10 x 4.8.
The frame is very handsome, and the whole in
perfect preservation. This picture has been
in my possession since my Mother's death,
whose desire it was that at my death it
should be presented to College of Physicians,
to be hung there. It seems to me expedient
to part with this much loved possession,
as I am past the three score years and ten
now, and would like to see it safely placed
during my life, besides we are leaving our
present residence to take up our new abode in
our Donegal home, where I am uncertain whether
there is a safe or suitable place to hang it,
and besides so immediately over the sea I am
I am afraid that there might be a risk to
the painting from the salt sea air. If the President and Council of the College of
Physicians will accept this picture and hang
it in a suitable place, I will give it, and
will have it safely packed and forwarded
about April 10th. Believe me to be
Yours
truly Mrs ) Rebecca Hime An early answer would greatly oblige. Macd.C
7.II.16
